behaviors.description: Exponential backoff doubles between consecutive failures, capped at configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: A pure unit test of `nextDelay()` can pass while the retry loop applies wrong state between attempts. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network needed. MOCK-HIDE: if transport is overly mocked but retry coordinator is real, failure is still observable. Minimum real boundary is retry coordinator + backoff policy wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run retry coordinator with a deterministic failing stub transport and fake clock. Assert observed scheduled waits are `base, 2x, 4x...` and then clamp at `maxBackoffMs` across real retry state transitions.

behaviors.description: Rate-limit responses are detected correctly and mapped to the correct wait time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit tests for parser or classifier alone may miss miswiring between HTTP response handling and retry scheduler. REAL-INFRA: no real network required. MOCK-HIDE: mocking 'already classified' errors would hide failures in response-shape detection.
behaviors.test_description: Feed realistic HTTP-like error responses (e.g., 429, retry-after headers, vendor body codes) through the client stack (response parser + retry logic). Assert only true rate-limit responses trigger retry and computed wait matches policy/header precedence.

behaviors.description: After waiting, the client resends the original request and returns success transparently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: A unit test can validate retry intent but miss actual resend of the original payload/options through the composed client path. REAL-INFRA: no subprocess/real API required. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing final return directly would hide resend defects.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub transport that fails first with rate-limit then succeeds. Capture request arguments per attempt and assert second attempt preserves original method/path/body/headers. Assert caller receives successful payload from `call()` without extra handling.

behaviors.description: After max retries, client returns typed `QuotaExhaustedError` instead of generic error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit-testing error class construction alone misses retry-limit boundary wiring. REAL-INFRA: no external system needed. MOCK-HIDE: mocking retry count outcome could hide off-by-one or wrong error mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Configure low `maxRetries`, force persistent rate-limit responses, execute full retry path, and assert rejection is instance of `QuotaExhaustedError` with expected typed fields (attempt count/metadata), not raw transport error.

behaviors.description: Full retry chain terminates and never loops indefinitely before surfacing error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit checks for loop counters can miss async scheduler interaction causing non-terminating retries. REAL-INFRA: still local process/timers. MOCK-HIDE: bypassing timer/retry executor would hide infinite-loop bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run full retry engine with always-rate-limited stub under fake timers and hard test timeout. Advance clock through all scheduled waits; assert promise settles exactly once after expected attempts and no further timers/attempts remain queued.

behaviors.description: Concurrent async callers in one process share one quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit-testing bucket math alone misses contention/race behavior across client instances/call sites. REAL-INFRA: no external API needed; local async concurrency is enough. MOCK-HIDE: isolated per-caller mocks would incorrectly pass while real shared-state wiring is broken.
behaviors.test_description: Start N concurrent `call()` invocations against a client configured with shared in-process quota state and controlled transport. Assert combined attempts/acceptance reflect a single global bucket (not per-caller buckets), including deterministic depletion behavior under contention.

behaviors.description: Queued waiting requests are dispatched in original submission order (FIFO).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit tests of queue data structure can pass while dispatcher integration reorders due to async scheduling bugs. REAL-INFRA: no real network needed. MOCK-HIDE: if send completion is mocked synchronously, ordering bugs may not appear.
behaviors.test_description: Submit multiple calls quickly while quota allows only one dispatch at a time; use controllable transport completion to keep queue occupied. Record actual send order at transport boundary and assert it matches enqueue order IDs exactly.
